reasoning: The Lean statement asserts the existence of a unique topology T' on X which (a) is itself a topology, (b) is contained in each T i, and (c) contains every topology that is contained in every T i — this exactly expresses "the unique largest topology contained in all T_α." The `is_topology` definition correctly encodes a topology (univ ∈ T, closed under binary intersections, closed under arbitrary unions, with ∅ arising from the empty union). The use of `∃!` is consistent with the informal "unique largest", since a largest element is automatically unique when it exists. Set containment `⊆` correctly represents "topology contained in a collection." No hypotheses are silently added or dropped.
